purpose: All knowledge dictionaries: Python standards, CLI stack, DB rules, use-case theory, real-world patterns

type_hints.collections: list[str] dict[str, int] not List Dict from typing

data_structures.structured: Pydantic or dataclass — never plain dict between functions
data_structures.enums: enum.Enum or enum.StrEnum — never string literals for categoricals

async.entry: asyncio.run() called once in cli.py only
async.http: httpx.AsyncClient always — never requests in async code
async.gather: asyncio.gather() for truly independent tasks only — never sequential pipelines

python.logging: logging module. Never print(). Lazy: logger.debug('%s', val) not f-strings.

python_cli.project_discovery: YAML via PyYAML optional dep. ucgen.yaml convention.
python_cli.db_for_local_state: SQLite stdlib sqlite3 or aiosqlite. No ORM for simple state.
python_cli.testing: typer.testing.CliRunner for CLI tests. pytest-asyncio asyncio_mode='auto'.

selection_table.condition: Structured relational data with foreign keys and joins
Local CLI or desktop app state, single user, simple schema
Document-heavy schema with variable structure, no complex joins
Graph relationships are primary query pattern, traversals
Full-text search is primary access pattern

pk_conventions.column_name: Always 'id' — never 'uuid' or '{table}_id' for the PK itself
pk_conventions.when_integer_ok: Internal lookup tables, enum-like tables only

general_rules: created_at and updated_at on every table
Soft deletes with deleted_at nullable — never hard delete user-generated content

use_case_theory.source: Cockburn (2001) Writing Effective Use Cases + IEEE 29148:2011

goal_levels.summary: Multiple sessions, multiple actors. e.g. Manage Customer Account. Use for scoping only.
goal_levels.user_goal: Single session, single actor achieves a goal. DEFAULT level for ucgen.
goal_levels.subfunction: Single step within a user_goal. Only extract if that step becomes too complex.

use_case_theory.stakeholder_rule: Always identify: primary actor (initiates), supporting actors (called), offstage stakeholders (affected but not present)

minimal_guarantee.definition: What system promises even when primary goal fails
minimal_guarantee.writing_rule: Think: what must be logged, recorded, or preserved regardless of outcome?

examples.BookAppointment: Attempt logged with timestamp and patient ID even if slot was taken
examples.RequestRide: Payment intent captured even if no driver found
examples.StreamSong: Play event recorded for royalties even if stream fails after 30s

use_case_theory.success_guarantee: Addresses each stakeholder's interest. Different from postconditions which describe system state.

scope_rules.keep_gui_out: Never describe interface elements. Write intent, not interaction.
scope_rules.step_count: 3-9 steps in main success scenario. >9 means extract a sub use case.

use_case_theory.extension_example: 2a: No available slots — System offers next available date and waitlist option
use_case_theory.asterisk_rule: *a: covers failures that can occur at any step e.g. session timeout, system down

entity_extraction.noun_rule: Extract every noun with identity and state — not just domain objects
entity_extraction.state_machine_signal: If normal course mentions status changes, primary entity has a state machine

scale_inference.inference: High write frequency — Cassandra or Redis for location
scale_inference.example: Uber: 500K writes/sec from driver location updates

scale_inference.trigger: Matching two parties in real time
scale_inference.example: Uber: driver must never be double-assigned

scale_inference.inference: Blob storage + CDN — never serve from app server
scale_inference.example: Spotify: audio via S3/CDN not API server

scale_inference.pattern: Webhook confirmation, never store card data

anti_patterns.anti: Single database for all use cases
anti_patterns.lesson: Uber split Trip, Location, Payment across services. Monolithic DB becomes bottleneck.
Spotify uses Kafka so analytics failure doesn't block stream start.
PCI compliance alone justifies Stripe. Never raw card data.

anti_patterns.anti: Scale from assumption not use case
anti_patterns.lesson: Uber's 500K writes/sec is derivable from 'Driver Updates Location' frequency × active drivers.
